quiz.title: Unit 4 Quiz 5, Grade 10 Biology
quiz.subtitle: Cell Biology: Advanced Organelle Functions
quiz.subject: Biology
quiz.topic: Organelle Functions, Prokaryotic vs. Eukaryotic Organelles, Membrane-bound Organelles

questions.question: Which of the following cell organelles is absent in prokaryotic cells? (Select all that apply)
options: Nucleus, Lysosome, and Endoplasmic Reticulum only
questions.explanation: Prokaryotes lack membrane-bound organelles. They do NOT have a nucleus, lysosomes, or endoplasmic reticulum. They DO have ribosomes (70S) and a plasma membrane.

questions.question: This organelle can be found inside the nucleus and produces ribosomes.
questions.explanation: The nucleolus is a dense region inside the nucleus where ribosomal RNA (rRNA) is synthesized and ribosome subunits are assembled.

questions.question: Where are proteins made in a cell?
questions.explanation: Ribosomes are the site of protein synthesis (translation). They can be free in cytoplasm or bound to rough ER.

questions.question: Which of the following cell organelles is primarily responsible for breaking down organic matter?
questions.explanation: Lysosomes contain hydrolytic enzymes that break down organic matter, old organelles, and pathogens. Peroxisomes break down fatty acids and detoxify harmful substances.

questions.question: Which of the following cell organelles is primarily responsible for storing food and other nutrients required for cell survival?
questions.explanation: Vacuoles store nutrients, water, and waste. Plant cells have a large central vacuole for storage and turgor pressure.

questions.question: ______________ is involved in the synthesis of phospholipids.
questions.explanation: Smooth ER synthesizes lipids, including phospholipids for membranes, and also steroids. Rough ER has ribosomes for protein synthesis.

questions.question: Which of the following cell organelles is present in plant cells but generally absent in animal cells?
questions.explanation: Chloroplasts are present in plant cells but absent in animal cells. Large central vacuoles are typical in plants but not entirely absent in animals (animals have smaller vacuoles).

questions.question: Which of the following cell organelles is present in animal cells but absent in plant cells?
questions.explanation: Centrosomes (containing centrioles) are present in animal cells and help organize microtubules during cell division. Plant cells lack centrosomes.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a double membrane-bound organelle?
questions.explanation: Endoplasmic reticulum is a single membrane-bound organelle. Chloroplasts, mitochondria, and the nucleus are all double membrane-bound.

questions.question: Which of the following statements is false about the nucleus?
options: It is called the brain of the cell
It contains DNA and other genetic materials
It is the site of ATP production
questions.explanation: ATP production occurs in mitochondria, not the nucleus. The nucleus contains DNA, controls cell activities, and is absent in prokaryotes.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You have a detailed understanding of organelle functions and distributions.
scoreMessages.70: Good job. Review which organelles are membrane-bound and their specific roles.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on organelle functions and plant vs. animal cell differences.
scoreMessages.0: Please review organelle functions, membrane-bound status, and cell type distributions.
